oracle.why: TWO reasons, both load-bearing. (1) HARNESS WAS BLIND: until #66, scripts/sim/boot.mjs left `usingRealTimeOffline` stuck true after load() — the game's offline-progress replay sets it (main.js:2901) and normally clears it via a setTimeout loop the sim stubs out. AT's mainLoop gates `setScienceNeeded()` + `autoLevelEquipment()` on `!usingRealTimeOffline`, so EVERY trace recorded against phase1-faithful was recorded with all gear-buying and science tracking disabled: zero buyEquipment events, U1 buyBuilding nearly absent. The L0 net was comparing a crippled AT against a crippled AT. (2) THE OLD ORACLE ENSHRINED A SHIPPED BUG: phase1-faithful contains #63 (needGymystic hardcoded true → scienceNeeded permanently +5,000,000). On 02-mid-u1 the old oracle computes scienceNeeded=5,001,452 and gathers science where the fixed build computes 1,452 and gathers buildings; every downstream buy timing cascades from that one number. The (save,index,fn) waiver mechanism is built for a few LOCALIZED fix divergences, not a wholly shifted trajectory (~130 brittle entries). Re-pinning was the correct move, and the v2 traces are strictly RICHER than the old ones (they contain the buyEquipment/buyBuilding events the old ones structurally could not), so the net is more sensitive after the re-pin than before it. NOTE: re-pinning is NOT routine — a naked oracle change is exactly the accidental-drift alarm this net exists to raise. Only re-pin behind a root-caused, reviewed, intentional behavior change.

notes.coverage: radon-only; the U1 corpus (01/02/03) never reaches RbuyJobs. The 04-u2-radon save (added #47) DOES run RbuyJobs — yet it produces ZERO L0 delta for THIS fixture's numeric window (verified 2026-07-10): L0 records buyJob(name, buyMax, force), and for a normal buy the fix only shifts the worker QUANTITY computed internally, not buyJob's args (buyMax=true carries no count). NOTE this is empirical, NOT a structural invariant: freeWorkers (jobs.ts:492) also feeds an `if (freeWorkers<=0) return` gate BEFORE any buyJob call, so a different U2 save/seed whose fixed-vs-buggy freeWorkers straddles 0 WOULD surface a real L0 divergence (missing buyJob calls that tick). For 04-u2-radon it stays well above the gate, so the fix remains gated by the jobs.RbuyJobs L1 regression (156→33), not an L0 waiver. This corrects the earlier prediction that adding a U2 save would force an L0 waiver here.
